description_long: This is a robust stone guard marker, featuring a two-tiered structure with a rectangular upper block set atop a rough, stacked stone base. The upper section is made of smooth, light-colored stone and displays a prominent inscribed plaque with Cyrillic text and a circular relief, likely commemorative in nature. The base is constructed from irregular, weathered stones, some of which are covered with moss and vegetation, giving the object an aged and historical appearance. The front face is the most ornate, with visible inscriptions and a portrait medallion, while the other sides are plain or show signs of weathering and discoloration. The object is solid and heavy, designed to serve as a protective or commemorative barrier in an urban or historical setting. Its overall appearance suggests it is both a functional obstacle and a monument.
description: A heavy, two-part stone guard marker with a smooth, inscribed upper block and a rough, mossy stone base, featuring a commemorative plaque and portrait medallion.
description_view_0: The front view shows the inscribed plaque with Cyrillic text and a circular relief, set on a smooth stone block above a rough, mossy stone base.
description_view_1: The left side reveals the plain, weathered surface of the upper block and the irregular, stacked stones of the base, with some moss visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays an unadorned, smooth stone block atop the rough stone base, with no visible inscriptions or decorations.
description_view_3: The right side shows some discoloration and weathering on the upper block, with the rough stone base and patches of moss at the bottom.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, along urban sidewalks, or near the corners of old streets, often marking boundaries or commemorating events.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on the edges of the stone base, discoloration and moss growth on lower stones, and some smoothing of the upper block's corners. These patterns are likely caused by prolonged weather exposure, occasional contact with vehicles or pedestrians, and natural aging.
attribute_historical_markings: The front face features an inscribed plaque with Cyrillic text and a circular relief medallion, likely commemorative. No other markings are visible on the other sides.
